directive.title: Neon DB connection and basic table creation

auditor.summary: The build provides a solid foundation with proper DECIMAL(15,4) financial precision, comprehensive audit triggers with SHA-256 checksums, and a well-structured migration system. However, it has critical gaps: the connection-pool.ts file is missing entirely, making it impossible to verify the 100 concurrent request handling requirement. The audit log immutability implementation uses deprecated RULEs instead of proper constraints. Error handling lacks specific coverage for network timeouts and connection limits. The cold-start requirement has no optimization or measurement mechanism. The build is recoverable with targeted fixes but requires attention before production deployment.

top_issues.description: Connection pool file is missing from generated code. The acceptance criteria requires handling 100 concurrent requests, but the pool config limits to 20 connections. Without the actual pool implementation, concurrent request handling cannot be verified.

Audit log immutability is implemented via RULEs which are deprecated in PostgreSQL and can be bypassed. The initial CHECK constraint (immutable_audit CHECK (false)) was correct but was dropped, leaving only RULE-based protection.
No cold-start optimization or warm-up logic. The acceptance criteria requires <500ms cold start but no mechanism ensures this. The fetchConnectionCache=true helps but doesn't guarantee the timing.
